scenario: Vertex Telco is building a Camunda 8.8 microservice that handles 6500 customers per day on a self-managed cluster. A senior engineer embeds the Zeebe client library into the microservice and wants to confirm the two fundamental things the application can do with that client, before sketching the BPMN model and worker glue code.
question: Which two things can an application do with the embedded Zeebe client library according to the best-practices guide?

options.text: Actively call Zeebe to start process instances, correlate messages, or deploy process definitions, and subscribe to tasks created in the workflow engine in the context of BPMN service tasks
Query the Zeebe internal database directly using SQL for reporting and patch process instance state in place when a corrective release ships across regulated environments
Embed the workflow engine in-process for unit testing and stream BPMN diagram changes to a connected Modeler instance from inside the client without involving any broker
Replicate process variables to a secondary broker for warm standby and elect a partition leader from the client when the current leader misses 3 heartbeats in a row

a.text: Correct. The best-practices page lists exactly two responsibilities: actively call Zeebe (start instances, correlate messages, deploy process definitions) and subscribe to tasks created for BPMN service tasks.

b.text: Incorrect. The client does not query a Zeebe internal database with SQL or patch instance state in place; it talks to the workflow engine over the documented APIs.

c.text: Incorrect. Embedded engine and Modeler streaming are not capabilities of the client library described on this page; Zeebe is a remote system.

d.text: Incorrect. Replication and leader election are responsibilities of the broker, not of an application using the client library.

explanation: The best-practices page calls out two fundamental things the application can do with the Zeebe client library: it can actively call Zeebe to start process instances, correlate messages, or deploy process definitions, and it can subscribe to tasks created by BPMN service tasks so that worker glue code is invoked when a job is created.

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
